auto|en|等3小时值了‼️终于拍到了电闪雷鸣的吉隆坡⚡️。原本计划在吉隆坡:
It’s worth waiting 3 hours‼ ️Finally took a photo of Kuala Lumpur with lightning and thunder⚡️. We originally planned to take photos of the sunset and blue night scenes from this location in Kuala Lumpur. However, we encountered a thunderstorm and had no choice but to try to take photos of lightning. 
The shooting process was difficult. When it rained in the restaurant, the windows were closed, so we could only shoot through the glass. This affected the image quality and there were reflections from the glass. If we forgot to bring a matte mask, we had to wrap the lens with clothes. Secondly, a tripod was not allowed, and the camera was not stable enough on a backpack. 
After 3 hours of time-lapse shooting, I finally captured the lightning moment. 
📍Skybar on the 33rd floor of Traders Hotel

auto|en|等3小时值了‼️终于拍到了电闪雷鸣的吉隆坡⚡️。原本计划在吉隆坡: It’s worth waiting 3 hours‼ ️Finally took a photo of Kuala Lumpur with lightning and thunder⚡️. We originally planned to take photos of the sunset and blue night scenes from this location in Kuala Lumpur. However, we encountered a thunderstorm and had no choice but to try to take photos of lightning. The shooting process was difficult. When it rained in the restaurant, the windows were closed, so we could only shoot through the glass. This affected the image quality and there were reflections from the glass. If we forgot to bring a matte mask, we had to wrap the lens with clothes. Secondly, a tripod was not allowed, and the camera was not stable enough on a backpack. After 3 hours of time-lapse shooting, I finally captured the lightning moment. 📍Skybar on the 33rd floor of Traders Hotel

auto|en|在吉隆坡最惬意的一小时，献给蒂蒂旺沙的日落。 傍晚从KLCG喝完: The most pleasant hour in Kuala Lumpur is dedicated to the sunset in Titiwangsa. I came here from KLCG in the evening after drinking coffee. It was still bright when I arrived, so I didn’t have to do anything. I sat down on the lawn and waited for the sky to change. The sky turned from foggy blue to orange pink, and the sunset covered the entire sky. The sunset slowly sank, and the city was slowly lit up, making it feel like time had slowed down. After dark, the Twin Towers light up on time, with the sparkling lake in front of you and the bright night view in the distance, your mood is completely healed! There is no need to rush the schedule or crowds, just sit quietly, stay from dusk to night, and see enough of the romance of Kuala Lumpur at once. 📍Titiwangsa Lakeside Park Taman Tasik Titiwangsa ✅ Free admission | Open 7:00-21:00 ✅ Best time: 18:00-19:30 (sunset + light up to see the whole thing)

auto|en|马来西亚湿地公园探险🐊 这个湿地公园位于马来西亚雪兰莪, 离KL: Malaysia Wetland Park Adventure🐊 This wetland park is located in Selangor, Malaysia, about 15 minutes' drive from KLIA Airport. It is a nature reserve and eco-tourism attraction. This 3,100-hectare wetland park was invested and constructed by the government to protect the natural ecological environment of the area. The main feature here is its water system, including lakes, swamps and rivers, which contain a wide variety of aquatic plants and wildlife. It is also a paradise for birds and insects, and visitors can enjoy a variety of waterfowl and rare birds, such as black swans and purple-breasted herons. In addition to viewing wildlife, it also offers a variety of outdoor activities such as boating, fishing, camping, hiking and horseback riding. Address: PAYA INDAH DISCOVERY WETLANDS CENTRE @ DISCOVERY PA

auto|en|本来只是路过想随便看看， 没想到在Johor Bahru被一家眼: I was just passing by and wanted to take a look, but I didn't expect to be surprised by an optical shop in Johor Bahru 📍Moment Optical Eco Botanic. It is not the kind of shop that is sales as soon as you enter, but more like a space where you can stroll around and try it out slowly. It is basic that there are many styles to choose from, but what impressed me more is that it feels a bit "beyond an optical shop". This is the first time I have come into contact with AI glasses at close range. The function is not just for "seeing clearly". It started to feel like a daily tool. There is another point that I think is a plus: the whole eye examination process is very smooth and fast, which is really friendly for people who don’t like to wait. Sometimes this kind of store visit is like this. There are no expectations, but it is easier to leave an impression.

There are not only Sky Mirrors here, but also Blue Tears, Rainbow Village, Silver Leaf Monkeys... - Recommended Attractions 📍Mount Royal Ticket: MYR 5 The commanding heights of Kuala Selangor, the second Sultan of Selangor once built a fortress here, which was later occupied by the Netherlands. Now only the lighthouse, fort, foundation stone and execution ground remain. In addition, there are macaques and silver leaf monkeys on the mountain that you can feed. Ps. Silver langurs are docile and cute, while macaques are wild. - 📍Sky Mirror

auto|en|勇敢让我们见识更广阔的世界 十年后 一个人 再一次踏入马来西亚: Braveness allows us to see a wider world. Ten years later, a person once again stepped into Malaysia and walked into Penang, a small town that is a World Heritage Site stamped by the United Nations. The colonial-style arcades and Chinese carvings create a unique beauty. The free time of walking on the streets. George Town is like a switch into the old times. The colorful houses, the murals hidden in the alleys, and the old streets full of graffiti feel like traveling back to the summer of the 1980s, and blending the Nanyang wind into the time. 📷Photography location: ❶Penang City

auto|en|总于尝到怡保很🔥的鸡蛋糕‼️ 一口下去味道是没有很惊艳，不过在鸡: Finally tasted the best chicken cake in Ipoh‼ ️ The taste is not very amazing when you take one bite, but it is a good choice in the world of chicken cakes [Like R] The fluffy chicken cake has a soft and fluffy texture and a very fragrant taste [Wow R] My favorite is the Pandan flavor, which is really super fragrant. When the box is opened, it is full of Pandan fragrance [色色R] [Wow R] The original flavor Mocha chocolate seems relatively ordinary. There is a thin layer of kaya sandwiched inside the chicken cake, and there is no kaya flavor at all.
The boss opened a stall in front of his house, selling only his signature mochi and peanut paste, priced at RM3.50 each. The peanut paste tastes very smooth, but it is not fragrant and has a bit of a burnt smell. The mochi is really glutinous, and the chopped peanuts are very fragrant! . The main deity enshrined in the temple, Fude Zhengshen, is the well-known Land Gong Zhang Fude, but he has always been revered as Da Bo Gong in the Nanyang region. Tua Pekong belief is the most common belief in Sarawak. There are Pekong temples in almost big cities and small towns to protect the people of the country.

auto|en|鱼饼 Keropok 登嘉楼最贵 Keropok 即是零嘴配料: Keropok is the most expensive fish cake in Terengganu. Keropok is both a snack ingredient and tableware. "To be honest, my fish cakes are very expensive because I only use freshly landed saury and sardines. Without these two fishes, I will not make them. I will never mix fish species or use trawl fish." He is one of the few remaining Chinese fish cake producers in Terengganu. It has been passed down for three generations and is a time-honored brand in the fish cake industry in Terengganu. Keropok fish cakes are fried fish cakes in maritime Southeast Asia.

auto|en|到售票处买门票+船票，一船往返200RM，可以找人拼船，一船最多: Go to the ticket office to buy tickets + boat tickets. One round trip is 200RM. You can find someone to share the boat with up to five people per boat. The first boat leaves at 8 o'clock, and the latest return trip is 3 pm, and the return journey must be done with five people. The disembarkation boat cannot stop at the shore, so it is best to take off your shoes and socks on the boat in advance. Go to the service office to get a map. There are many hiking routes. If you want to return by boat before 3 o'clock on the same day, you can choose 2-3 routes depending on your physical strength. The staff suggested us three trails: 4, 5, and 6. On Trail 6, you can see the weathered rocks and beaches engulfed by the waves from the cliff, and you can climb down to the beach. The one-way journey is 2.6km, and you will see unique landforms along the way, including coral reefs hidden on the seabed 26 million years ago. You can sit on them and feel the charm of time. The water on the road turns red and yellow due to the iron content, and there are pitcher plants of different shapes everywhere, making this hike full of fun.

auto|en|📍无招牌藏在 SS2一楼的日式干拌拉面小店 ，适合半夜想来一碗温: 📍A small Japanese dry ramen shop hidden on the first floor of SS2 without a sign, perfect for when you want to have a bowl of warm and simple Japanese food in the middle of the night 🍜 The newly opened shop is run by a couple who hope to bring back the familiar taste of noodles they have eaten in Japan. 📌 Premium TKG, RM 18.90 A very healing bowl of hot rice mixed with raw eggs, topped with barbecued pork, salted kombu, and shredded green onions. The noodles have an al dente texture but are a little springy. The soy sauce base is a bit salty, but I like salty taste so I can accept it. The barbecued pork was a bit dry and could be more tender; the eggs were a bit overcooked and I would have liked them a little more runny. 📌 Nik

auto|en|马六甲一日游，这四个地方千万别错过👇 📍荷兰红屋 东南亚最老的荷: Don’t miss these four places during a day trip to Malacca 👇 📍The Red House is the oldest Dutch building in Southeast Asia, with a history of more than 400 years. It is recommended to wear light-colored clothes when taking photos. The red walls make people look white. 📍The Victoria Fountain is directly opposite the Red House, a small British-style fountain. Even though it is not big, with the clock tower behind it, it looks European in style 📍Portugal Ancient City Gate There is only one gate left, but this is the gate with the most stories in Malacca. The locals call it the "Lucky Gate". Remember to touch it for good luck when taking pictures🍀📍The former site of St. Paul's Church has to climb a small slope, but the view from above is amazing. The entire Strait of Malacca has a panoramic view. It is most beautiful when going at sunset. ✅Route: Red House → Fountain → Ancient City Gate → Church, the whole journey can be reached on foot, and it is not tiring to finish it in half a day.

auto|en|百年前华人在这里淘金，如今以猫山王闻名——你不知道的劳勿故事 🌧: A hundred years ago, Chinese gold mined here, and now it is famous for Musang King - the story of Raub that you don’t know 🌧️ Raub in the rain: a small town prosperous because of labor. From the moment we arrived in Raub, the rain never stopped. From the pouring rain to the dense pattering, this small town in Pahang was silent in the rain. A hundred years ago, countless workers from southern China bent over washing tin rice in the river, buried their youth in the mines, and sent their homesickness back to Tangshan. A piece of dusty labor history is hidden in the rain in front of us. 🏚️

Labuan is in Sabah, Malaysia, close to Brunei. Labuan means accepting people from southern Fujian. There are shipwreck dive sites here, which overlap with Brunei shipwreck dive sites. Wreck Diving + Duty-Free Island If you like niche diving sites with rare treasures and beautiful scenery. Labuan is a place worth visiting. Labuan is in Sabah, Malaysia, close to Brunei. Labuan means accepting people from southern Fujian. There are shipwreck dive sites here, which overlap with Brunei shipwreck dive sites. But the sea actually belongs to Malaysia, and what you see here is the material of the most beautiful Cement Wreck cement ship. Wreck diving is only allowed for experienced divers with "AOW" and at least 50 dives. Due to the depth, it is recommended to do 2 wreck dives a day. We went to the coral reef for the third dive. Since local diving is still in the recovery stage, it is recommended to go at least in a small group to facilitate arrangements. Labuan is still an offshore duty-free island, and everything on the island is very cheap. Bruneians like to come here to spend money on weekends. The price of 8+1 is half that of Guangzhou.
